name: Instagram News Core
headlineRules: Lead with the strongest consequence, discovery, or warning.
Prefer simple, concrete wording over abstract hype.
Make the first line feel like a reason to stop scrolling.
Avoid unsupported clickbait and broad claims.
subheadlineRules: Use the second line to add one clarifying detail.
Keep it short enough to survive a template fit-check.
Do not repeat the exact words from the first line.
captionRules: Summarize the key finding in 3-5 short sentences.
Keep the tone journalistic but human.
Do not invent quotes, numbers, or medical claims.
imageDecisionRules: Use the original image when it is relevant, human, and visually clear.
Recommend a generated replacement when the source looks like a logo, thumbnail, infographic, icon, or weak stock asset.
If the original image is weak, still keep the post publishable and provide an image prompt for a future image generator.
imagePromptTemplate: Editorial Instagram cover for news story: {{title}}. Focus on the key scene or consequence. Realistic, high contrast, clean composition, no text, no watermark.

examples.rawTitle: New study links late-night scrolling to poorer sleep quality
examples.headline: SCROLLING HURTS SLEEP

examples.rawTitle: Scientists identify simple diet change that improves gut health
examples.headline: DIET CHANGE HELPS GUT
